text: The migration workflow shall provide a clear Application Programming Interface (API) to handle the migration process and default configuration.
The migration API shall require the inclusion of only one header file.
The user shall provide a migration configuration explicitly.
The user shall be able to provide metadata related to the conversion for storage in the converted files.
The system shall store automatically generated migration metadata in the converted files upon request.
The migration workflow shall be able to call external tools and services.
The migration process shall use persistent unique global identifiers for records access.
The system shall use an external tool to scan files before conversion.
The migration API shall allow external tools to be used for file format identification.
The ARC to WARC migration tool shall be able to run on multiple machines.
The system shall allow logging during the migration to be toggled on or off at any time.
The system shall provide software checkpoints during processing for managing atomic transactions.
The system shall be able to perform a dry-run migration.
The system shall find and report WARC records with duplicate checksums.
The validation tool shall use metadata in the WARC files to match records with their corresponding ARC files.
The system shall use payload checksum comparisons for core validation.
The validation tool shall provide a sampling option to validate a random percentage of records.
The system shall use an external ARC reader to double check the conversion.
The system shall be able to perform a round trip validation by migrating WARC files back to ARC.
The repackaging tool shall filter records based on URL regular expressions.
The repackaging tool shall filter records based on MIME-Types.
The repackaging tool shall filter records based on size.
The repackaging tool shall filter records based on timestamp.
The repackaging tool shall filter records based on any field in the WARC specification.
The repackaging tool shall allow pre and post record file and job operations.
Each repackaged WARC file shall include a user-defined metadata record describing the extraction context.
The migration framework shall provide tools to build reports from WARC files.
The system shall export summaries in XML or CSV formats.
The WARC browser shall display aggregated WARC summaries in the user interface.
The WARC browser shall display a manifest of WARC files and their locations.
The system shall provide a diff tool to compare WARC sets based on defined criteria.
The system shall provide a tool to draw difference graphs between WARC collections.
The system shall provide a method to view crawl deltas for quality assurance.
The system shall implement WARC browser server-side rewriting.
The system shall integrate a proxy mode inside the WARC browser.
The system shall integrate the full-text search module with the WARC browser.
The tools shall be able to process a set of multiple WARC files simultaneously.
The tools shall scale to process large collections using distributed processing and data transport.
The tools shall offer best possible performance to correctly process large collections.
The tools shall be able to run on multiple machines.
The tools shall avoid unnecessary technology dependencies.
The tools shall be compliant with Java development environments.
The tools shall provide logging facilities to follow command progression.
The tools shall provide shell script wrappers for major commands to enhance usability.
The tools shall be able to operate in modes that restrict effects to selected collection subsets.
The workflow system shall have a configurable management strategy for migration from ARCs to WARCs.
The migration workflow system shall be driven by command line tools and scripts.
The system shall provide a Web User Interface to build migration configurations.
The system shall provide a Web User Interface to monitor pause and restart active migrations.
The system shall provide pre and post conversion actions at each level of the migration.
